recommendations.evidence: ISF non-linearity (EXP-2511): typical correction dose 2.5U is 56% less effective per unit than 1U (power-law β=0.9). Splitting into 2×1.2U would be ~87% more effective total.
recommendations.rationale: Correction doses above 1.5U show diminishing returns. At 2.5U, each unit achieves only 26 mg/dL drop vs 60 mg/dL at 1U. Consider: (1) splitting large corrections into smaller doses spaced 30+ min apart, (2) using ISF=26 for doses ≥2U. This is a pharmacokinetic property (β=0.9), not circadian.
